citation: Brandon, R., Costello, D., Porter, M., & Strang, R. (2024). RTRT - Zines - Resource Document. Google Docs. https://docs.google.com/document/d/1XFpG96vwsOzRS7rcTeqKx6FK15EwkI6A5B0gvtf7Qv0/edit?usp=sharing&usp=embedfacebook
summary:
This item is a five-page Round Table Round Table resource document for a session on Zine Month, Zine Quest, tabletop RPG zines, zine-making resources, and TTRPG safety tools. The document lists panelists Russell Brandon, Danielle Costello, Molly Porter, and Rebecca Strang, all of whom bring library, teaching, programming, GameRT, game collection, board game night, Dungeons & Dragons, or youth gaming experience to the topic. It then gathers links rather than presenting a sustained article, study, or program report.

The first substantive section introduces Zine Quest and Zine Month as tabletop RPG zine movements, pointing to Kickstarter, Zine Month, Crowdfundr, and explanatory articles from Dicebreaker, Tabletop Gaming, and Medium. The document then lists current and past RPG zines such as *Beak, Feather, & Bone*, *Rolling with the Youth*, *Stargems*, *Wandlore*, *Mazes RPG*, *No Nazis in Valhalla*, *Outliers*, and many others. This makes the sheet useful for librarians who want to understand small-press RPG publishing, tabletop design culture, and zine-based game materials.

The resource also includes non-gaming zine resources from ZineLibraries.info, Vice, multiple academic-library LibGuides, and museum or university zine-making guides. These links emphasize that zines are already a library collection, preservation, programming, and instruction format, even outside games. For GBLS, that matters because RPG zines connect game creation, self-publishing, fandom, local authorship, analog making, and collection development. Zine-making can function as a game-design workshop format or as a low-cost path into tabletop RPG creation.

The most directly GBLS-relevant portion is the safety-tools section. It states that TTRPGs can cover a wide variety of topics and themes and that safety tools help participants pause, take breaks, and address uncomfortable situations. The linked tools include Script Change, Lines and Veils, The Door is Open, the TTRPG Safety Toolkit and guide, and *Consent in Gaming*. The document also includes the GameRT Twitch policy, which sets expectations for kindness, respect, anti-hate-speech moderation, harassment reporting, and private messaging.

Despite those useful pointers, this item should not be added to `review.md`. It is a resource sheet, not a piece of literature that documents a library zine program, analyzes RPG zine collections, evaluates a game-design workshop, or studies TTRPG safety tools in library settings. It is best understood as professional scaffolding for future program design. If the GBLS project later includes an appendix or toolkit, this document could help identify materials for RPG zine workshops, self-publishing events, TTRPG collection development, or safety-policy preparation. In the main literature review, stronger sources should carry the claims about TTRPG programming, game creation, inclusion, and safety.
sourceText:
ROUND TABLE ROUND TABLE Topic: Zine Month!
Links This doc: https://bit.ly/4bwn1U6 Recording:
Panelists Russell Brandon (he/him) is the Data Services Librarian at the Fant Memorial Library and the Director of the Kossen Center for Teaching & Learning at the Mississippi University for Women. He teaches in the Digital Studies program and promotes data literacy on campus. Before coming to The W, Russell taught psychology at Mississippi State University and has previously worked as a middle school computer teacher. He thinks play is pretty much the best way to learn
anything. More at russellbrandon.info.

Danielle Costello (she/her) is an academic librarian at University of Georgia where she teaches library instruction and develops programming for the student body. She is also President-Elect for ALA’s Games and Gaming Roundtable where she helps to develop programming and resources to bring games and play into all libraries. Outside of work she moonlights as a cleric and occasionally as a capybara trying to maintain a healthy low stress high mayhem lifestyle.
Molly Porter (she/her) is the Adult and Teen Services Librarian at the West Feliciana Parish Library in Saint Francisville, Louisiana. She created the library’s board game collection, and she hosts board game nights and runs Dungeons and Dragons and other TTRPGs for their patrons. Molly has an MA in English literature, an MLIS, and a certification in archival studies from Louisiana State University. She serves on the Awards Committee and co-chairs the Programming Committee for the ALA Games and Gaming Round Table. In her free time, she plays as many games as her cat will allow.
Rebecca Strang (she/they) is a Children’s Services Librarian at Naperville Public Library in Illinois where she runs monthly gaming programs like Family Game Night, Video Game Club, Pokemon Club, and Adventurers’ Club (a kids RPG club). She is also the Past President of GameRT and has previously taught Game Studies at Loyola University Chicago. Rebecca believes play and storytelling are essential and strives to carve places for both in her communities. In her free time, she hangs out with her partner and their cats, plays games, sends snail mail, and drinks tea. Find out more at https://toplayishuman.com/.
RESOURCES
What is Zine Quest / Zine Month (ZiMo)? ● Kickstarter Zine Quest: https://www.kickstarter.com/zinequest ○ Zine Quest 2024: Kickstarter’s Annual Open Call for RPG Zines Returns in February ● Zine Month: https://zinemonth.spread.name/?page=1 ● Crowdfundr: https://crowdfundr.com/spotlight/tabletop-nonstop/
Related Articles or Blogs
● Carter, C. (2024, February 1). February’s month-long celebration of tabletop RPG zines returns - here’s how you can join in. Dicebreaker. https://www.dicebreaker.com/categories/roleplaying-game/news/zine-quest-zine-mo nth-2024-introduction-explainer
● Tabletop Gaming (2024, January 3). What is Zine Month & Zine Quest?. Tabletop Gaming. https://www.tabletopgaming.co.uk/features/what-is-zine-month-zine-quest
● Hollon, S. (2023, October 20). How Zine Quest Could Remake the Tabletop RPG Landscape: Insights from food trucks and Kickstarter data. Medium. https://medium.com/theuglymonster/how-zine-quest-could-remake-the-tabletop-rpg -landscape-857c16acf43e

Zines to Check Out ZineQuest 2024 ● Beetle Knight ● Campanions: A TTRPG Inspired by Video Game Companions ● Dungeons! | A Mörk Borg Supplement [Conversion] ● Field Agent Handbooks: Observancy Dept. 1924–28 ● Grotten - 1 Bit Deeper ● HIC: Tapestry - Toilet Roll Paper Game ● Jukebox: The Karaoke Musical Tabletop Roleplaying Game ● The Last Ride of the Rustwyrm ● The Magus & The Oracle - Solo RPG & Inspiration Deck ● Making a Tabletop RPG for YOUR Particular Kid ● MÖRK KÖPHETS: A Dead Mall Dungeon Crawl for MÖRK BORG ● Mystery Under Magi-Mart ● No Nazis in Valhalla ● Outliers: the award-winning solo TTRPG of weird research ● Radish Quest ● Sentai & Sensibility RPG ● Tabletop Gone Mad: No-Prep Fill-in-the-Blank TTRPG One-Shots ● TAPE ● Thrifty Trades of Fey ● Tiny Fables ● The Tower - A System Agnostic Puzzle Dungeon Crawl ● A Tower Made of Sky ● Wandlore: A Crafty Witch TTRPG for 1-4 Players ● Witch's Cat: A Solo RPG for ZineQuest ● Wither & Grow ● Wurm
Zines to Check Out from Previous ZineQuest ● Beak, Feather, & Bone: A Map-Labeling RPG ● Beneath Hallowed Halls - A Dark Academia PBTA Zine ● Be Seeing You ● A Butterfly Dies ● A Complicated Profession ● Eat Trash. Be Free. ● Fruit of Law ● i’m sorry did you say street magic ● Lighthouse at The Edge of the Universe ● Low Stakes ● Mazes RPG - New Zines every 3 Months

● The Merchant ● Pigeon’s Eleven ● Pixie Punks - A unique TTRPG of fey gone bad #ZineQuest5 ● Psychic Trash Detectives ● Rolling with the Youth: A guide to running inclusive, safe, and fun tabletop-roleplaying
games for all ages ● Sanctuary & Sentinel ● Stargems: A TTRPG for Young Adventurers (ages 4+) ● Teenage Mutant Dirtbags ● The Very Good Dogs of Chernobyl ● A Visitor's Guide to The Rainy City

https://library.pugetsound.edu/zines/making ● Dajska, Emma (2012, March 1). How to Make a Zine.
https://www.rookiemag.com/2012/05/how-to-make-a-zine/ ● Du Val, S. (2024, February 7). Whitworth Library's Zine Collection. Whitworth University

https://guides.library.cornell.edu/zines101/home ● Institute of Contemporary Art / Boston. (2020, May 28). Make Your Own Mini-Zine.

California State University Pollak Library LibGuides. https://libraryguides.fullerton.edu/zines ● LSC-North Harris Library (2023, November 28). Making Zines. LSC-North Harris Library LibGuides. https://nhresearch.lonestar.edu/Zines ● Simmons University Library. (2024, January 24). Zines!. Simmons University Library LibGuides. https://simmons.libguides.com/zines ● Stahura, D. (2024, January 17). Zines. Salem State University Library LibGuides. https://guides.library.cornell.edu/zines101/home ● Tinio, J. (2024, January 17). Loud on Paper Library Guide. Purdue University Libraries and School of Information Studies LibGuides. https://guides.lib.purdue.edu/loudonpaper/home

Videos
● The History of Zines with Kate Bingaman-Burt ● Let's Talk About Zines and Why You Should Make One ● How To Make A TTRPG Zine! ● Zine Month 2024 ● Zine History Playlist
Safety Tools These are the safety tools used during GameRT’s TTRPG streams. TTRPGs can cover a wide variety of topics and themes. Safety tools allow us to work through those potentially tough topics with comfort and grace, and they enable us to pause, take breaks, and address uncomfortable situations.
● Script Change by Beau Jágr Sheldon ● Guest Blog: An Introduction to Lines and Veils ● The Door is Open by Eirik Fatland
○ Blog by Lizzie Stark ○ Blog 2 by Eirik Fatland ● TTRPG Safety Toolkit collected by Kienna Shaw and Lauren Bryant-Monk ○ TTRPG Safety Toolkit ○ TTRPG Safety Toolkit Guide ○ Google Drive ● Consent in Gaming by Sean K. Reynolds and Shanna Germain
Game RT Twitch Policy 1. Be kind and respectful to one another 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason 4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please notify a GameRT twitch mod and one of us will assist you 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct message
Learn More About GameRT Blog: bit.ly/GameRTBlog Facebook: bit.ly/GameRTLeague & bit.ly/GameRTFacebook Instagram: https://bit.ly/GameRTInstagram Resources: bit.ly/GameRTPublicResources Twitch: https://bit.ly/TwitchGameRT Website: bit.ly/GameRTWebsite Zotero: https://bit.ly/GameRTZotero Questions? Email gamert@ala.org
